review_title: The tab that is constantly open
description: My note-taking became smoother than ever. I used to have Evernote back in the day so I don't honestly know how much Nimbus Note is better than them. I tried Notion and I felt Nimbus Note AI to be better and feel better. About Nimbus Note, I love how I have all my thoughts in one place. It's like a borrowed brain, to uncluttered mine.
author: Pedro P.
pros: As a copywriter, I spend far too much online doing "research." And it's easy to stumble upon something I believe might be useful in the future. I have tones of draft emails, text files on my computer, random notes on my phone... And do you know what happened to all those notes? Me neither. Because I always lacked structure and organization to know where to find them when I needed them. With Nimbus Note, my note-taking is like a well-rehearsed orchestra. I know where things are. The tags, folders, and workspaces make everything so smooth. I can add pictures or documents. I absolutely love the outline option where I can immediately see what's inside each note, making it easy to access it. Right now, it's the only tab that gets to stay open throughout my day.
cons: When adding a table on a note, I wish I could freeze the top line or something. Just like we do on a normal spreadsheet. Other than that, the workspaces, especially at the beginning, can be a bit tricky. More than once I thought I've lost all my work just to find it in a different workspace. It can get your heart to run faster, but everything ends up being fine.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: More useful than you could ever imagine.
description: The best NOTES platform out there. Better than Evernote, which has been stagnant. Nimbus helps you note down everything without being lost in all the notes because of its organized and structured nature. You can unclutter your mind and prioritize your focus. It also has many valuable features that are not usually in a "Notes" app, but they are!
author: Alberto M.
pros: It has so many valuable features. And they can also be used with its chrome extensions. Being able to embed practically anything into my notes has been so convenient. The mobile app helps a lot too. It's way better than my native notes app and other paid services. Better note taking, better ease of use, better notes management, Papa John's.
cons: Needs integration with Grammarly. UI can still be improved for better note-taking and while on a smaller window.

description: Overall for a free and quick extension, I would definitely recommend it. It's easy to use, it's easy to generate a link of the screenshot or video recording so that you can share it with someone, and it's free! If you're looking for something free, simple, easy to use, and something quick for your work then this is definitely the tool that you need.
author: Yoselin T.
pros: Nimbus has pretty much all of the features that you can imagine and need. I've been using Nimbus for work for quite a few years now and it has definitely helped to have a quick and free extension to record videos and take screenshots. With one simple click, you get a drop-down list with all of the features that they provide. It's very easy to use.
cons: The only downside of this extension is that there are times where the tools get stuck? Also, you can't really take a proper screenshot if there are two scrollable fragments in a single page, and sometimes when I do manage to take a screenshot of a scrollable page when I upload it, it looks like it has been cut off or something. I'm not too sure if these are bugs but it can be a little bit inconvenient when having a lot of workloads.

description: Excellent at organizing my notes for my current books I'm writing, and journals. I love the ability to use it across all my devices without any penalties whatsoever.
author: Leon B.
pros: I love the folder structure I love the ability to pick what format each block of text. Ability to write infinite notes Ability to record videos on the fly (alternative to Loom) Ability to screencapture AND edit with arrows and other editing features 100's of templates available to start my notes! (so cool)
cons: when using smaller-sized windows, I must scroll horizontally to view the rest of my text. A bit annoying, but I am sure they will update something in the future. The android app seems kind of clunky and unnatural at first - I think i just have to get used to it
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Manu D.
pros: I am a former Evernote user and what I love most about Nimbus is the ability to create workspaces. I now have a dedicated space for each of my clients and I can share it with them without them being able to see the rest of the notes. The screen capture, text or video recording tools are incredibly powerful too and offer options that have become indispensable to me (being able to capture a web page in its entirety and not just the part visible on the screen for example). The price/performance ratio is just incredible. I also love the ability to integrate external elements into a note as "Embed". I use it for Teleportme and for my google docs.
cons: What annoys me a little is the slowness and lack of precision of the research. The passage from one workspace to another is sometimes a little slow too. But i am pretty sure the team will improve this soon.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: happy with the product, probably worth mentioning it's not the only product that we use by Nimbus.
author: Ninos M.
pros: It's a very good and valid alternative to another software that we consider to be industry standard.clean user interface.doesn't require "training" for any client to use.Creating workspaces and sharing them is a good feature , when we work on roadmaps or tasks that should be shared with others.
cons: Speed!seems to be "slow" especially on mobile phone, the platform could use an upgrade in terms of response time.

review_title: Makes life easier, able to take notes anywhere!
description: After switching from Evernote, i did not have to regret for once, they provide everything i can expect, their customer support is very up to date, easy interface, awesome data management and many more,
author: Khan Md Mohaiuminul Islam S.
pros: As a civil engineer, i need to collect many information, sometimes its very hard to use different kinds of note book, Previously i was using evernote, after switching to Nimbus note, I can easily create my notes and organize them as my choice. I am able to find my docs , anytime. From anywhere! their customer support is awesome.
cons: i really don't have any big complain about Nimbus note, They Are Continuously improving themselves, they need to work on syncing , i hope they can make better workplace experience for us.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Frank D.
pros: This has a lot of looks and features kind of like ever now. But, what is better is it has a ton of features that are much more intense than ever now. I really enjoy being able to have a folder in a folder. This has changed the game for how I organize my files.
cons: I definitely got on the software early on so there are some bugs they're working out and sometimes the website can be a little bit slow. But there have been steady improvements over the last several months.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: In world full of distractions, Nimbus Note become our must have note taking app.
description: We're quite satisfied with the Nimbus Note and they're constantly developing and improving it. Their support is also good.
author: Sumeet M.
pros: It's super simple and easy to join the Nimbus and get started. We're successfully able to import all our notes from the Evernote(sorry Evernote).
cons: They seriously need to work on the UI UX as Evernote Web Clipper is super amazing. Secondly, it mostly didn't work while taking full-page screenshots.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Stephen C.
pros: Support is off the charts good. At times I’ve gotten email response in minutes, felt like seconds. The tool itself is wildly flexible, and an awesome replacement to my other note taking tools. The chrome add ons take it over the top as a win.
cons: Checklists are too rigid to supplant a to do list, even tho there is a separate “to-do” list attached to each note. It is a bit of a wasted feature and a tease because it doesn’t provide any additional functionality than one you can create yourself in the note via checkboxes. As a project manager I’ve found that to be confusing and disappointing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It all started great, I could import thousands of notes from my evernote and file them easily in the same way. Then I installed the browser extension and the desktop app. That's where I felt like the tool was still good value for the money but not the best on the market. You lose quite some time waiting for the apps to sync and retrieve content, wether it's the web app, the browser extension or the desktop app.
author: Geoffrey J.
pros: Nimbus note has a lot of features under the hood to help you file and format your notes in the best possible way (private and business workspace, folders, sub-folders, tags... And a lot of options within the note like different headings, import files, copy images, create tables, etc...). The "import from evernote" feature worked flawlessly.
cons: 1) It's very slow sometimes. You're typing your note and your app will freeze (web app or desktop app : same issue). I never had this problem with evernote. 2) It's very slow to sync. 3) Results aren't presented with the most recently created/edited files first. And then you have to keep scrolling through dozens of notes until you find the one you created yesterday... 4) The browser extension takes forever to capture a full page. Evernote did this very quickly in comparison. 5) Trying to save a note from my android phone (with the "share > Nimbus" feature) is cumbersome. You have to put the focus back to title to add a title on your note, it's not prefilled by default, no auto-tagging, just a link to the page, no content capture. Don't get me wrong, it's still great value for the price. But there is still a long way to go until it's on par with evernote.

review_title: Don't Overlook this Feature Rich & Affordable Product
description: Nimbus Notes is a feature rich not taking app, that goes far beyond its simple note taking feature. It is well supported and the team continue to bring out new features.
author: Asif A.
pros: Lots of features. With few restrictions . Multiple projects and easy to collaborate.
cons: From a styling side of things, it can look a little cheap. I particularly don't care much for the emojis , . It

review_title: a simple tool for complex projects
description: I'm happy with the product and the customer service. When I was confused with the cname configuration for the portal, the Fusebase team invited me in for an onboarding call and helped me set up the product the same day. I guess this kind of customer treatment should be the golden rule for product companies
author: Dimitrii K.
pros: So far, Fusebase is the best software for managing multiple clients. I really like the unified link feature because it automatically directs each client to their portal, so i highly recommend it to anyone seeking versatility and an intuitive UI to work with people with different backgrounds.
cons: Works really well for me. Would like to see more widgets for surveys, i think with Fusebase automations it will shine

description: Well, it is just splendid. I am actually now using several note-taking tools: Nimbus Note, Evernote and Google Docs, but for some specific things which you need to be able to access quickly, create some drafts in one moment and save, it is probably the best solution.
author: Olga B.
pros: I really like that it is easily accessible, adaptive, affordable, adjustable to the needs of every user, appealing and aesthetical. Besides, it is really user-friendly, useful and ultramodern. What's more, this note-taking tool is practical, powerful and helps you effectively keep your promising drafts. I enjoy this software more than just using some editors because MS Office and LibreOffice sometimes may be really slow-working, despite using good hardware, enough physical memory space, order and no bugs in your OS. Nimbus Web is responsive, reliable and really remarkable. It is absolutely not costly, and this thing is stylish and detail-sensitive. Word search is almost flawless, docs downloading is fast, notes ordering is easy-to-use and apply. You can easily add any tags, change fonts or wordings coloring, add to favorites or remove them, create tidy folders or clean them, restore the notes from trash, name and rename them. You can even create to-do-lists, share things, set reminders, or even upload some files. You can quickly print out note, search through notes, find something in a note. It is really a pragmatic, neat and just professional thing which I always need.
cons: I dislike that I cannot change my email easily in my account. They offer to write to their support team, but it is just some extra hassle and I would want to have a reliable and quick way to change my email. Besides, sometimes it is working slowly in some browsers (especially in Chromium, OS Ubuntu Budgie 18.04.)

description: My experience with Nimbus Note was quite positive. Its versatility and comprehensive note-taking capabilities provided a convenient and organized way to manage my information. The synchronization and offline access features ensured that my notes were always available across devices. While there were occasional performance issues, they did not significantly hinder my overall experience. With some improvements in performance and user interface, Nimbus Note has the potential to become an even more powerful and user-friendly note-taking solution.
author: Marek T.
pros: One of the things I liked most about Nimbus Note was its versatility and feature-rich nature. The ability to create and organize various types of notes, including text, images, audio, and even web clippings, made it a comprehensive solution for capturing and managing information. The seamless synchronization across multiple devices and the availability of offline access ensured that my notes were always accessible whenever and wherever I needed them. Additionally, the powerful search functionality and customizable tags made it easy to find and retrieve specific information quickly.
cons: While Nimbus Note had many positive aspects, one thing I liked least was the occasional performance issues, especially when dealing with large note collections or attachments. There were times when it took longer than expected to sync or load notes, which could be a bit frustrating. Additionally, the user interface, although functional, could benefit from some refinements to enhance the overall user experience and make it even more intuitive and visually appealing.

description: My experience with Nimbus Note has been highly positive. It's revolutionized the way we manage information, collaborate with clients, and organize our work. Its intuitive knowledge base has made information retrieval efficient, while its client collaboration features have fostered transparency and productivity. Any initial challenges were quickly overcome, leaving us with a tool that has become essential to our daily operations.
author: Anastasiia T.
pros: I appreciate how Nimbus Note effortlessly centralizes knowledge management, enabling seamless information sharing and collaboration. Its user-friendliness and accessibility stand out, streamlining our organizational process.
cons: To be honest, there's nothing specific that comes to mind that I disliked about Nimbus Note. Its benefits have clearly overshadowed any minor inconveniences, making it a valuable asset for our team.

review_title: Keeping our customers in the loop!
description: Overall I am very satisfied with this tool. I used to use another tool similar but they continued to crash or request upgrading to continue with the same functions I had become accustomed to. Nimbus has been very reliable and I often suggest it to my clients.
author: Venita T.
pros: This tool is very useful in my everyday campaign management. I use it to keep track of my projects and their successful outcomes can easily be shared with my clients. Also love that I can use it in incognito to search for results in any location, very helpful with clients across the USA and Canada.
cons: Honestly, I have not found any part of the tool to be hard to learn or teach someone else how to install and use.

review_title: A Nimbus that even Harry Potter would be jealous of.
author: Matthew J.
pros: I was actually turned onto Nimbus a couple of years ago when I needed to screenshot an entire email template for a client. I used to try to stitch together multiple screenshots to make it work and that's when a coworker told me about Nimbus. I can quickly and easily screenshot anything and everything, as well as record video, upload it to my account and share a link with all to see. I can even CNAME it so that it's branded. Wonderful! The team behind Nimbus is constantly pushing out updates and has added new products to its core offering that are amazing as well.
cons: There's absolutely nothing I don't like about Nimbus. I wish there were some minor updates to the video recording aspect, but they've already said this is in the works and will actually deployed soon, so if that's my only pain point and it's already addressed, then that leaves...nothing!

review_title: Get Organized and Innovate with Nimbus Note
description: Describe your overall experience with Nimbus Note
author: Swapnil B.
pros: What sets Nimbus Note apart from other note-taking apps is its integrated text editor. It's incredibly flexible and user-friendly, allowing you to easily add pictures, tables, hyperlinks, and various lists in your notes. Plus, it syncs seamlessly across all devices, making it possible to access your notes from anywhere. All of these features combined make it one of the best note-taking apps out there.
cons: One of the least liked features that I noticed with Nimbus Note was its lack of native integration with other note-taking apps. For example, if you use Evernote or Google Docs for your note-taking needs, you would have to manually copy and paste content from one app to another in order to share notes across platforms. This is a bit inconvenient for users who prefer their notes to be stored and accessible across multiple devices.

description: I am happy to use the application thanks to its Chrome extension, offline use and easy use. Although it has some development features, I can say that it is a 6/10 application.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I use Nimbus mostly via chrome extension. I find it very successful in offline use and note management. I recommend the add-on feature of Nimbus to all my friends. While working through the browser, I take short notes to Nimbus.
cons: Actually, I don't use every feature of Nimbus. I think that note management and content scanning feature need to be developed more. With a little development, they can include me to use it outside of the chrome extension.

review_title: So many use cases - a must have
description: Nimbus fills so many voids for us - many of which we didn't even know existed. Rather than writing an email, we put the information in a Nimbus note, file it in the appropriate folder and share it. No longer can people say 'I never received that email'. And then in terms of as an event hub it is a godsend. You create your notes in two folders - private for your staff team and public for everyone to see. You suddenly have all the internal information and a handy guide for your audience in one place, well presented and incredibly easy to update.
author: Robbie F.
pros: Its flexibility. Whilst we signed up for a note taking app, it turned into so much more. We use it for assigning briefs to staff, client portals, event hubs and general project management spaces.
cons: From time to time its speed isn't the quickest, but there has been vast improvements on that front, so we aren't too concerned. When it comes to the tables it lacks a couple of basic functions like pinning the first column/row and resizing all rows/columns. A couple more tweaks to add more flexibility to its public page presentation would take them to the next level.

review_title: Been using for years and absolutely love it!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: -Ability to brand your own Evernote type system - Create personal accounts for friends & family - Notion vibe
cons: It can be slow sometimes and freeze occasionally. I noticed it about 3x so far within the month.

review_title: I am a Nimboss with Nimbus
description: I am too pumped up with the tool and I use it almost daily. Rather I would say that I start my day with Nimbus and also End my day with Nimbus. It has become a spinal cord for my day to day work and I would highly recommend it to everyone.
author: aka R.
pros: I love the fact this amazing note-taking web app has a fully syncing android and ios app. Nimbys are just so amazing as it is not just a note-taking app, rather it can do much more. Some of the most unbelievable and well-tought features of nimbus are, the ability to share my notes publicly like a webpage and also add a layer of security to it by adding a password to it. Also, I really feel happy to see how well they make use of emoji in our notes and the possibility to organize our notes with folders and tags.
cons: Every tool or software has cons and Nimbus has cons too. But as I say the company is very open to feedback and they implement the needed changes as early as possible if they see it to be fit. Expecting Nimbus to make it a bug-free tool within a few months after looking at the pace of the development and improvement of the whole app and user experience.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I like to use it for almost everything! Before I used notepad++ and Word to remember thought/ideas and different Apps to manage my ToDos. Now I always think "Nimbus" first. It is such a great way editing thoughts in a structured manned on the fly! It is so easy and intuitive moving notes around (e.g. from folder "bugs" to "solved")! I like and make heavy use of the option to share Notes and folders! A great way building a manual and sharing it with either an individual contact or in general (on the company website) as key documentation. Your "Nimbus Help & Guides" was a great source for ideas! Besides all that documentation and note taking use cases, I also collect links/bookmarks and complete articles or fragments! So easy to put it into the correct folder and tag it!
author: Hans N.
pros: My favorite: How easy it is to include Gifs! Next is the wysiwyg editor, the nice mobile apps, general ease of use, excellent options to organize things (workspace, folders, many levels of subfolders), ton of features. And of course the excellent documentation!
cons: Impossible sorting (defining the order of) workspaces and notes manually! Sporadic not working article captures (only at the beginning of my work; probably just bad luck ;-) ). I miss a general search feature for the help & guides; and a direct link to the forum on your website (or I simply couldn't find it yet).
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Alexey K.
pros: FuseBase's client engagement tracking has been quite beneficial. It allows us to see how clients interact with the content we create, providing valuable insights into their preferences and behaviors. The platform's ability to handle campaign feedback and revisions in one centralized location has also been a major plus, saving us time and effort in coordinating with clients. Additionally, the ease of sharing campaign analytics and reports directly with clients through the portal has enhanced our transparency and accountability.
cons: Actually all work pretty great, I like the way the platform is flexible, so nothing to worry about or complain
